title: Dichondra microcalyx
taxonomy: Dichondra microcalyx is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It belongs to the genus Dichondra, which is a group of annual herbs native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Dichondra microcalyx places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Solanales. This species is often found in moist habitats and is characterized by its small, white flowers. Further classification of Dichondra microcalyx may include its specific placement within the kingdom and the subcategories of the plant kingdom.

intro: Dichondra microcalyx, also known as Mexican clover or Dichondra, is a perennial herb native to Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress.
